---
title: "Fire_NOBO_Manuscript_2022 (reviewed)"
output: html_document
date: "2026-04-24"
editor_options:
  chunk_output_type: console
---

```{r setup, include=FALSE}
knitr::opts_chunk$set(echo = TRUE)
```

## Libraries

```{r}
library(janitor)
library(tidyverse)
library(lubridate)
library(readxl)
library(writexl)
library(sf)
library(lme4)
library(betareg)
library(MuMIn)
library(performance)
theme_set(theme_minimal())
```

## Named constants

```{r}
# All hardcoded dates / thresholds live here so sensitivity analyses are trivial.
BURN_DATE       <- ymd("2022-04-11")
FEED_START_DATE <- ymd("2022-06-06")
FEED_START_DSF  <- as.integer(FEED_START_DATE - BURN_DATE)  # 56
BOOT_B          <- 2000                                     # bootstrap reps
BOUND_EPS       <- 1e-5                                     # beta bound
ACF_LAG         <- 7                                        # weekly lag for Ljung-Box
# Telemetry coordinates are UTM; Ichauway is in zone 17N. If a future
# deployment reports a different zone, set POINTS_CRS here.
POINTS_CRS      <- 26917                                    # NAD83 / UTM zone 17N
```

## Helper functions

```{r}
# Bound proportions away from 0 and 1 for beta regression.
bound_beta <- function(x, eps = BOUND_EPS) pmin(pmax(x, eps), 1 - eps)

# Bootstrap prediction CIs for a betareg model, by case resampling.
# Per-iteration seeds make the procedure reproducible even if a fit fails
# mid-loop (failed iterations don't desync the RNG stream).
boot_beta_ci <- function(formula, data, newdata, B = BOOT_B, seed = 123) {
  preds <- matrix(NA_real_, nrow = B, ncol = nrow(newdata))
  for (i in seq_len(B)) {
    set.seed(seed + i)
    d <- data[sample.int(nrow(data), replace = TRUE), ]
    fit <- tryCatch(
      suppressWarnings(betareg(formula, data = d, link = "logit")),
      error = function(e) NULL
    )
    if (!is.null(fit)) {
      preds[i, ] <- tryCatch(
        predict(fit, newdata = newdata, type = "response"),
        error = function(e) rep(NA_real_, nrow(newdata))
      )
    }
  }
  data.frame(
    lo = apply(preds, 2, quantile, probs = 0.025, na.rm = TRUE),
    hi = apply(preds, 2, quantile, probs = 0.975, na.rm = TRUE)
  )
}

# Block bootstrap for time-series-aware CIs. Use when ACF shows residual
# autocorrelation — case resampling would produce CIs that are too narrow
# because it ignores temporal structure.
boot_beta_ci_block <- function(formula, data, newdata, block_size = 7,
                               B = BOOT_B, seed = 123, time_var = "DSF") {
  data  <- data[order(data[[time_var]]), ]
  n     <- nrow(data)
  nblk  <- ceiling(n / block_size)
  starts_all <- seq_len(max(1, n - block_size + 1))
  preds <- matrix(NA_real_, nrow = B, ncol = nrow(newdata))
  for (i in seq_len(B)) {
    set.seed(seed + i)
    starts <- sample(starts_all, size = nblk, replace = TRUE)
    idx <- unlist(lapply(starts, function(s) s:min(s + block_size - 1, n)))
    idx <- idx[seq_len(n)]
    d   <- data[idx, ]
    fit <- tryCatch(
      suppressWarnings(betareg(formula, data = d, link = "logit")),
      error = function(e) NULL
    )
    if (!is.null(fit)) {
      preds[i, ] <- tryCatch(
        predict(fit, newdata = newdata, type = "response"),
        error = function(e) rep(NA_real_, nrow(newdata))
      )
    }
  }
  data.frame(
    lo = apply(preds, 2, quantile, probs = 0.025, na.rm = TRUE),
    hi = apply(preds, 2, quantile, probs = 0.975, na.rm = TRUE)
  )
}

# Leave-one-out CV for a single-predictor betareg model. Evaluates fit
# against the raw (unbounded) PIB when available.
beta_loocv <- function(data, formula = pib ~ ps, truth_col = "pib_raw") {
  n <- nrow(data)
  preds <- numeric(n)
  for (i in seq_len(n)) {
    fit <- suppressWarnings(
      betareg(formula, data = data[-i, ], link = "logit")
    )
    preds[i] <- predict(fit, newdata = data[i, , drop = FALSE], type = "response")
  }
  truth <- if (truth_col %in% names(data)) data[[truth_col]] else data$pib
  list(
    rmse = sqrt(mean((truth - preds)^2)),
    cor  = cor(truth, preds)
  )
}

# Temporal autocorrelation diagnostics for a betareg fit. Observations must
# be in time order. Returns Pearson residuals invisibly.
check_autocorr <- function(model, label = "", lag = ACF_LAG) {
  r <- residuals(model, type = "pearson")
  cat("\n--- Autocorrelation diagnostics:", label, "---\n")
  print(Box.test(r, lag = lag, type = "Ljung-Box"))
  acf(r, main = paste("ACF of Pearson residuals:", label))
  invisible(r)
}

# Minimal manuscript-friendly plot theme.
plot_theme <- theme(
  legend.position   = "none",
  panel.grid.major  = element_blank(),
  panel.grid.minor  = element_blank(),
  axis.line         = element_line("black"),
  axis.ticks        = element_line("black"),
  axis.text         = element_text(colour = "black", size = 12),
  axis.title        = element_text(colour = "black", size = 12),
  legend.title      = element_text(colour = "black", size = 12),
  legend.text       = element_text(colour = "black", size = 12),
  plot.margin       = margin(5, 10, 5, 5, "pt")
)
```

## Load data

```{r}
summer22 <- read_xlsx(
  "/Volumes/Samsung_T5/BOBWHITE_DATA/Clean/SummerLocations/Summer2022/summer_2022_points_veg_CorrectBirdsOnline.xlsx"
) %>%
  mutate(date = ymd(date)) %>%
  filter(status == "A")

# Burn compartment polygons (for distance-to-edge calculations).
burn_polys <- st_read(
  "/Volumes/Samsung_T5/BOBWHITE_DATA/Maps/2022_burn_compartments/Polygons/NoAG_NoHW/2022_burn_units_NoAG_NoHW.shp"
)

# Daily rainfall, Crafton weather station, 2022.
rain <- read_xlsx("/Users/grayum.jeffrey/Downloads/Crafton_rainfall_2022 .xlsx") %>%
  clean_names() %>%
  transmute(
    date          = ymd(date),
    rain_total_mm = rain_total_mm,
    dsf           = as.integer(date - BURN_DATE)
  )
```

## Build PIB

```{r}
# Audit: days with zero or missing birds_online. These are NOT days with
# PIB = 0; they are days with no valid denominator and must be dropped.
bad_days <- summer22 %>%
  filter(is.na(birds_online) | birds_online == 0) %>%
  distinct(date, birds_online)
cat("Rows with birds_online NA or 0:", nrow(bad_days), "\n")
if (nrow(bad_days) > 0) print(bad_days)

# Unique birds in burned compartments each day.
birds_burned_daily <- summer22 %>%
  filter(burned == 1) %>%
  group_by(date) %>%
  summarise(birds_in_burned_areas = n_distinct(band_numb), .groups = "drop")

# Days with zero birds in burned areas legitimately give PIB = 0. Days
# missing a denominator are dropped (not imputed to 0).
s22 <- summer22 %>%
  left_join(birds_burned_daily, by = "date") %>%
  mutate(birds_in_burned_areas = replace_na(birds_in_burned_areas, 0)) %>%
  filter(!is.na(birds_online), birds_online > 0) %>%
  mutate(pib = birds_in_burned_areas / birds_online)

stopifnot(all(is.finite(s22$pib)))
stopifnot(all(s22$pib >= 0 & s22$pib <= 1))
```

## Distance-to-edge

```{r}
# CRS handling: telemetry `easting` / `northing` are projected metres. We set
# a known UTM CRS on the points and reproject the burn polygons into it
# before computing distances — never coerce a blank CRS onto the points.
stopifnot(!is.na(st_crs(burn_polys)))    # burn polys must carry a CRS
s22_sf <- s22 %>%
  st_as_sf(coords = c("easting", "northing"), crs = POINTS_CRS, remove = FALSE)
burn_polys_proj <- st_transform(burn_polys, POINTS_CRS)
burn_edges      <- st_boundary(burn_polys_proj)

# Nearest-edge distance for every point, in metres.
s22$distance_to_edge <- as.numeric(apply(st_distance(s22_sf, burn_edges), 1, min))

# Reference lines in the histograms are computed from the data, not hardcoded.
mean_in  <- s22 %>% filter(burned == 1) %>% summarise(m = mean(distance_to_edge)) %>% pull(m)
mean_out <- s22 %>% filter(burned == 0) %>% summarise(m = mean(distance_to_edge)) %>% pull(m)

# DSF vs distance to edge, birds within burn units only.
s22 %>%
  filter(burned == 1) %>%
  ggplot(aes(DSF, distance_to_edge)) +
  geom_vline(xintercept = FEED_START_DSF, linetype = "dashed", color = "midnightblue") +
  geom_point() +
  labs(x = "Days since fire", y = "Distance to perimeter of burn unit (m)") +
  theme_bw() + plot_theme

# Mixed model: does distance-to-edge change with DSF within burned units?
# NOTE: random intercept per bird does not absorb within-bird temporal
# autocorrelation. For a published estimate, refit with an AR(1) correlation
# structure (nlme::lme(..., correlation = corAR1(form = ~ DSF | band_numb))).
dist_model <- lmer(distance_to_edge ~ DSF + (1 | band_numb),
                   data = filter(s22, burned == 1))
summary(dist_model)

# Mean distance to edge by burn status.
s22 %>%
  group_by(burned) %>%
  summarise(mean_distance = mean(distance_to_edge), .groups = "drop")

# Histogram: birds inside burns (reference line = observed mean).
s22 %>%
  filter(burned == 1) %>%
  ggplot(aes(distance_to_edge)) +
  geom_histogram(binwidth = 10, fill = "midnightblue", alpha = 0.8) +
  geom_vline(xintercept = mean_in, linetype = "dashed", color = "gray") +
  labs(x = "Distance to edge", y = NULL,
       title = sprintf("Birds within burn compartments (mean = %.1f m)", mean_in))

# Histogram: birds outside burns.
s22 %>%
  filter(burned == 0) %>%
  ggplot(aes(distance_to_edge)) +
  geom_histogram(binwidth = 20, fill = "midnightblue", alpha = 0.8) +
  geom_vline(xintercept = mean_out, linetype = "dashed", color = "gray") +
  labs(x = "Distance to edge", y = NULL,
       title = sprintf("Birds outside burn compartments (mean = %.1f m)", mean_out))

# Boxplot comparison.
s22 %>%
  mutate(burned = factor(burned, labels = c("Unburned", "Burned"))) %>%
  ggplot(aes(burned, distance_to_edge)) +
  geom_boxplot(fill = "midnightblue", alpha = 0.5) +
  labs(x = "Burn status", y = "Distance to edge")
```

## Day-level dataset

```{r}
# Veg is constant within a day — verify before slice_head collapses rows.
invariance <- s22 %>%
  group_by(DSF) %>%
  summarise(
    n_pib  = n_distinct(pib),
    n_pg   = n_distinct(pg),
    n_ps   = n_distinct(ps),
    n_week = n_distinct(week),
    .groups = "drop"
  )
violations <- filter(invariance, n_pib > 1 | n_pg > 1 | n_ps > 1 | n_week > 1)
if (nrow(violations) > 0) {
  warning("Within-day values vary on the following DSF; slice_head will silently pick one row:")
  print(violations)
} else {
  cat("Within-day invariance check: PASSED\n")
}

s22_daily <- s22 %>%
  arrange(DSF, date) %>%                     # deterministic slice_head
  group_by(DSF) %>%
  slice_head(n = 1) %>%
  ungroup() %>%
  arrange(DSF) %>%                           # ensure time order for ACF/Ljung-Box
  select(pib, DSF, week, start_date_of_week, pg:hs) %>%
  mutate(
    feed    = as.integer(DSF >= FEED_START_DSF),
    pib_raw = pib,                           # retain unbounded value
    pib     = bound_beta(pib)                # bounded copy for betareg
  )

# Boundary diagnostic. If many days sit at 0 or 1, a zero/one-inflated beta
# (gamlss::BEINF or glmmTMB) is more principled than bounding.
p0 <- mean(s22_daily$pib_raw == 0)
p1 <- mean(s22_daily$pib_raw == 1)
cat(sprintf("Proportion PIB == 0 (raw): %.3f\n", p0))
cat(sprintf("Proportion PIB == 1 (raw): %.3f\n", p1))
if (p0 > 0.05 || p1 > 0.05) {
  message("WARNING: >5% of days sit at a boundary. Consider a ZOIB model before trusting inference.")
}

s22_pre  <- filter(s22_daily, feed == 0)
s22_post <- filter(s22_daily, feed == 1)

# Overall PIB over time with supp-feed marker.
ggplot(s22_daily, aes(DSF, pib_raw)) +
  geom_point() +
  geom_vline(xintercept = FEED_START_DSF, linetype = "dashed", color = "midnightblue") +
  labs(x = "Days since fire", y = "PIB", title = "2022") +
  plot_theme
```

## Vegetation correlations

```{r}
veg_cor_22 <- s22_daily %>%
  select(pg:hs) %>%
  cor() %>%
  round(2)
veg_cor_22

veg_cor_22 %>%
  as.data.frame() %>%
  tibble::rownames_to_column("variable") %>%
  write_xlsx("/Volumes/Samsung_T5/FIRE_NOBO_MANUSCRIPT/veg_cor22.xlsx")
```

## PIB ~ shrub cover (ps): separate pre- and post-supp models

```{r}
fit_and_plot_ps <- function(dat, title) {
  m <- betareg(pib ~ ps, data = dat, link = "logit")
  print(summary(m))
  check_autocorr(m, label = title)

  grid <- data.frame(ps = seq(min(dat$ps), max(dat$ps), length.out = 1000))
  grid$pred <- predict(m, newdata = grid, type = "response")
  grid <- cbind(grid, boot_beta_ci(pib ~ ps, dat, grid, B = BOOT_B))

  p <- ggplot(grid, aes(ps)) +
    geom_point(data = dat, aes(ps, pib_raw), size = 2, alpha = 0.7) +
    geom_line(aes(y = pred), linewidth = 1.2) +
    geom_ribbon(aes(ymin = lo, ymax = hi), alpha = 0.2) +
    labs(x = "Percent shrub cover", y = "PIB", title = title) +
    plot_theme
  print(p)

  invisible(list(model = m, grid = grid))
}

pre_ps  <- fit_and_plot_ps(s22_pre,  "2022: Pre-supplemental period")
post_ps <- fit_and_plot_ps(s22_post, "2022: Post-supplemental period")

# LOOCV evaluated against the raw (unbounded) PIB.
loocv_pre  <- beta_loocv(s22_pre)
loocv_post <- beta_loocv(s22_post)
loocv_pre
loocv_post
```

## Rainfall joined to daily data

```{r}
# Daily cumulative rainfall since the burn date is the rainfall variable used
# for modeling. Weekly totals are retained only for descriptive plots.
rain_cum_daily <- rain %>%
  arrange(dsf) %>%
  mutate(cum_rain_mm = cumsum(rain_total_mm)) %>%
  select(dsf, cum_rain_mm)

week_lookup_22 <- s22_daily %>%
  distinct(week, start_date_of_week) %>%
  arrange(start_date_of_week) %>%
  mutate(
    start_date = as.Date(start_date_of_week),
    end_date   = lead(start_date) - 1
  )
week_lookup_22$end_date[is.na(week_lookup_22$end_date)] <-
  week_lookup_22$start_date[is.na(week_lookup_22$end_date)] + 6

rain_weekly_22 <- rain %>%
  select(date, rain_total_mm) %>%
  tidyr::crossing(week_lookup_22) %>%
  filter(date >= start_date, date <= end_date) %>%
  group_by(week, start_date_of_week) %>%
  summarise(weekly_rain_mm = sum(rain_total_mm, na.rm = TRUE), .groups = "drop")

s22_rain <- s22_daily %>%
  left_join(rain_cum_daily,  by = c("DSF" = "dsf")) %>%
  left_join(rain_weekly_22,  by = c("week", "start_date_of_week")) %>%
  arrange(DSF)

stopifnot(all(!is.na(s22_rain$cum_rain_mm)))
stopifnot(all(!is.na(s22_rain$weekly_rain_mm)))

# Descriptive plots (weekly bars, cumulative curve).
ggplot(s22_rain, aes(week, weekly_rain_mm)) +
  geom_col(fill = "midnightblue") +
  labs(x = "Week", y = "Weekly rainfall (mm)", title = "2022 (descriptive only)") +
  plot_theme

ggplot(s22_rain, aes(DSF, cum_rain_mm)) +
  geom_area(fill = "midnightblue", alpha = 0.95) +
  labs(x = "Days since fire", y = "Cumulative rainfall (mm)", title = "2022") +
  theme_bw(base_family = "Times New Roman", base_size = 12) +
  theme(legend.position = "right",
        panel.grid.major = element_blank(),
        panel.grid.minor = element_blank(),
        axis.line  = element_line("black"),
        axis.ticks = element_line("black"),
        axis.text.x  = element_text(colour = "black", size = 12),
        axis.title.y = element_text(colour = "black", size = 12),
        axis.title.x = element_text(colour = "black", size = 12),
        legend.title = element_text(colour = "black", size = 12),
        legend.text  = element_text(colour = "black", size = 12),
        axis.text.y  = element_text(colour = "black", size = 12),
        strip.background = element_blank(),
        strip.text = element_text(colour = "black", size = 12),
        plot.margin = margin(t = 5, r = 10, b = 5, l = 5, unit = "pt"))
```

## Collinearity audit (printed for the record)

```{r}
# Weekly rainfall is the modeled rainfall variable: cum_rain_mm is monotonic
# in DSF (r ~ 0.97), so it is structurally a time proxy. weekly_rain_mm is
# not monotonic — any signal it carries is a genuine rainfall effect.
cor_mat <- s22_rain %>%
  select(ps, feed, weekly_rain_mm, DSF) %>%
  cor() %>%
  round(2)
cor_mat
```

## Beta-regression candidate set, ranked by AICc

```{r}
# Three univariate candidates plus two multivariate candidates combining the
# two predictors that are NOT collinear in this dataset (ps vs weekly_rain_mm
# correlate at r = 0.26 — well below the ~0.7 threshold where collinearity
# destabilises a betareg fit). DSF is excluded because it is structurally a
# time axis rather than an ecological predictor and correlates at r > 0.85
# with ps and feed.
m_ps        <- betareg(pib ~ ps,                  data = s22_rain, link = "logit")
m_rain      <- betareg(pib ~ weekly_rain_mm,      data = s22_rain, link = "logit")
m_feed      <- betareg(pib ~ feed,                data = s22_rain, link = "logit")
m_ps_rain   <- betareg(pib ~ ps + weekly_rain_mm, data = s22_rain, link = "logit")
m_ps_x_rain <- betareg(pib ~ ps * weekly_rain_mm, data = s22_rain, link = "logit")

# Autocorrelation diagnostics on each candidate.
walk2(
  list(m_ps, m_rain, m_feed, m_ps_rain, m_ps_x_rain),
  c("ps", "weekly_rain_mm", "feed",
    "ps + weekly_rain_mm", "ps * weekly_rain_mm"),
  ~ check_autocorr(.x, label = .y)
)

# For single-predictor models we print the predictor's p-value; multivariate
# models get NA here because the table is single-slot. Full coefficient
# tables are printed below for each multivariate fit.
extract_fit <- function(model, name, p_row = 2) {
  sm <- summary(model)$coefficients$mean
  p  <- if (!is.na(p_row) && p_row <= nrow(sm)) sm[p_row, "Pr(>|z|)"] else NA_real_
  data.frame(
    Model = name,
    R2    = unname(performance::r2(model)$R2),
    AICc  = MuMIn::AICc(model),
    p     = p
  )
}

summary22_rain <- bind_rows(
  extract_fit(m_ps,        "PIB ~ ps"),
  extract_fit(m_rain,      "PIB ~ weekly_rain_mm"),
  extract_fit(m_feed,      "PIB ~ feed"),
  extract_fit(m_ps_rain,   "PIB ~ ps + weekly_rain_mm", p_row = NA),
  extract_fit(m_ps_x_rain, "PIB ~ ps * weekly_rain_mm", p_row = NA)
) %>%
  arrange(AICc) %>%
  mutate(
    deltaAICc = AICc - min(AICc),
    weight    = MuMIn::Weights(AICc)
  )

summary22_rain

# Coefficient summaries for the multivariate models so all slopes are visible.
cat("\n--- PIB ~ ps + weekly_rain_mm (2022) ---\n")
print(summary(m_ps_rain)$coefficients$mean)
cat("\n--- PIB ~ ps * weekly_rain_mm (2022) ---\n")
print(summary(m_ps_x_rain)$coefficients$mean)

write_xlsx(summary22_rain,
           "/Volumes/Samsung_T5/FIRE_NOBO_MANUSCRIPT/model_summary_22_rain_fixed.xlsx")
```

## Prediction plot: top model (PIB ~ ps * weekly_rain_mm), dry vs wet

```{r}
m_top <- m_ps_x_rain
summary(m_top)
check_autocorr(m_top, label = "top model: ps * weekly_rain_mm")

# Two representative weekly-rainfall levels: 10th (dry) and 90th (wet) percentile.
rain_levels <- quantile(s22_rain$weekly_rain_mm, probs = c(0.10, 0.90))
print(rain_levels)

# Prediction grid: ps spans observed range; weekly_rain_mm fixed at each level.
ps_seq <- seq(min(s22_rain$ps, na.rm = TRUE),
              max(s22_rain$ps, na.rm = TRUE),
              length.out = 200)
newdat <- expand.grid(ps = ps_seq, weekly_rain_mm = rain_levels)
newdat$pred <- predict(m_top, newdata = newdat, type = "response")

# Block-bootstrap CIs (block_size = 7 days) on the interaction predictions.
newdat <- cbind(
  newdat,
  boot_beta_ci_block(pib ~ ps * weekly_rain_mm,
                     s22_rain, newdat,
                     block_size = 7, B = BOOT_B, time_var = "DSF")
)

# Discrete dry/wet labels for the legend.
newdat$rain_band <- factor(
  ifelse(newdat$weekly_rain_mm == rain_levels[1],
         "Dry (10th %)", "Wet (90th %)"),
  levels = c("Dry (10th %)", "Wet (90th %)")
)

ggplot(newdat, aes(ps, pred, color = rain_band, fill = rain_band)) +
  geom_ribbon(aes(ymin = lo, ymax = hi), alpha = 0.20, colour = NA) +
  geom_line(linewidth = 1.2) +
  scale_color_manual(values = c("Dry (10th %)" = "#e76f51",
                                "Wet (90th %)" = "#2a9d8f"),
                     name = "Rainfall level") +
  scale_fill_manual(values  = c("Dry (10th %)" = "#e76f51",
                                "Wet (90th %)" = "#2a9d8f"),
                    name = "Rainfall level") +
  labs(x = "Percent shrub cover",
       y = "Predicted PIB",
       title = "2022") +
  theme_bw(base_family = "Times New Roman", base_size = 12) +
  theme(legend.position = "bottom",
        panel.grid.major = element_blank(),
        panel.grid.minor = element_blank(),
        axis.line  = element_line("black"),
        axis.ticks = element_line("black"),
        axis.text.x  = element_text(colour = "black", size = 12),
        axis.title.y = element_text(colour = "black", size = 12),
        axis.title.x = element_text(colour = "black", size = 12),
        legend.title = element_text(colour = "black", size = 12),
        legend.text  = element_text(colour = "black", size = 12),
        axis.text.y  = element_text(colour = "black", size = 12),
        strip.background = element_blank(),
        strip.text = element_text(colour = "black", size = 12),
        plot.margin = margin(t = 5, r = 10, b = 5, l = 5, unit = "pt"))
```
